prompt: A haunting monochrome Japanese animated movie poster in gritty ink-wash and charcoal style, high contrast black, white, and gray only, with a tall vertical composition. The scene is viewed from inside a dark room toward an open doorway. On the right stands a young boy in silhouette, seen from behind in three-quarter view, with short messy hair, wearing a black-and-white horizontal striped T-shirt, loose dark pants, and sneakers, quietly staring out through the door. The left side shows the open wooden door itself, rough and weathered, with a visible round knob and a vertical line of Japanese text painted on it that reads 「開けたのは、ぼくじゃない」. Beyond the doorway is a surreal towering mountain or jagged monolith, centered in the distance, painted like a hand-drawn ink illustration, with a glowing white zigzag path or crack snaking from the base to a bright opening near the summit. Small floating rock fragments hover around the mountain. At the foot of the mountain, exactly 4 tiny human silhouettes stand together on a dark ridge facing it. The bright doorway light spills into the room and transforms into a long black shadow stretching toward the viewer across the wooden floor; at the end of that shadow is a demonic face shape with exactly 2 white almond-shaped eyes embedded in the darkness. The floorboards and walls are crosshatched and heavily textured, creating an eerie psychological horror atmosphere. Add Japanese festival-style laurels and release copy on the upper right in 3 stacked award blocks with elegant serif typography, plus large release date text reading “8.28 ROADSHOW”. Place the film title huge across the lower middle in distressed white hand-painted letters: “RAISE”, with smaller katakana beneath it reading 「レイズ」. Add a Japanese tagline centered along the bottom above the credits: 「君の『影』が、世界を救う。」 Include dense, tiny Japanese credit lines and a website at the very bottom, styled like a real theatrical poster. Overall mood: ominous, poetic, surreal coming-of-age horror fantasy, indie anime film poster, dramatic negative space, cinematic poster design, unsettling but beautiful.
